name: Ayla K.
bio: Traditional Kurdish performer known for preserving ancient folk melodies passed down through generations in the Zagros mountains.

name: Bahar D.
bio: Contemporary Kurdish artist blending traditional motifs with modern arrangements. Active in the Dohuk cultural scene.

name: Chnar M.
bio: A virtuoso santoor player from Sulaymaniyah, Chnar has revived many classical Kurdish compositions for solo instrument.

name: Delal R.
bio: Kurdish vocalist and composer who fuses traditional Kurdish music with jazz and electronic elements.

name: Farhad S.
bio: Master of Persian classical radif and a celebrated tar player from Tehran. Has performed internationally for over three decades.

name: Gohar T.
bio: Armenian duduk player specializing in sacred and liturgical music from the Geghard Monastery tradition.

name: Hesen Z.
bio: Kurdish tanbur master from Kermanshah province. Hesen's music draws from Yarsan spiritual traditions.

name: Jinan A.
bio: Syrian-Kurdish vocalist who emerged from the Qamishli underground scene. Known for her powerful, emotive delivery.

name: Kamran P.
bio: Kamran is a renowned kamancheh player and composer whose works bridge Persian classical music with contemporary composition.

name: Leyla Q.
bio: Leyla is a baglama virtuoso from Diyarbakir who has dedicated her career to documenting and performing Anatolian folk songs.

name: Miran K.
bio: Miran is a classically trained violinist from Erbil who adapts Kurdish folk melodies for string ensemble performances.

name: Nergiz P.
bio: Nergiz is a singer-songwriter from Qamishli whose lyrics explore themes of exile, memory, and hope through a modern Kurdish folk lens.

name: Osman T.
bio: Osman is a dengbêj (traditional Kurdish storyteller and singer) from Mardin who has performed oral epics for over forty years.

name: Parvin Sh.
bio: Parvin is a master of the ney, specializing in the radif of Mirza Abdollah. Her interpretations are both meditative and technically brilliant.

name: Qadir H.
bio: Qadir is a Kurdish composer and oud player who blends maqam traditions with ambient electronic soundscapes.

name: Rojin B.
bio: Rojin fronts a Kurdish folk-rock band from Amed, known for electric saz riffs and politically charged lyrics.

name: Soran M.
bio: Soran is a pianist and composer from Slemani who reimagines Kurdish classical pieces through Western classical forms.

name: Tanya R.
bio: Tanya is an Armenian-Kurdish vocalist from the Jazira region whose warm alto brings ancient hymns to new audiences.

name: Umid G.
bio: Umid is a zurna and mey player from Urfa who has performed at weddings and festivals throughout southeastern Turkey.

name: Vesta S.
bio: Vesta is a santoor prodigy from Isfahan whose polyphonic arrangements push the boundaries of Persian classical repertoire.

name: Welat D.
bio: Welat is a dengbêj master from the Serhat region who preserves the epic tradition of Kurdish oral history through song.

name: Xelef T.
bio: Xelef is a tanbur player and Yarsan priest from the Guran region who transmits sacred spiritual repertoire.

name: Yazdan P.
bio: Yazdan is a setar player and composer from Mashhad who integrates Persian classical music with minimal contemporary composition.

name: Zahra K.
bio: Zahra is a Kurdish vocalist from Kermanshah specialized in the repertoire of female Kurdish storytellers known as 'kilam'.

name: Arsham S.
bio: Arsham is a kamancheh player and improviser from Tabriz whose work draws from both the Azerbaijani mugham and Persian radif traditions.

name: Hesen Zîrek
bio: Kurdish singer and composer (1921-1972), widely considered one of the greatest voices in Kurdish music history. Known for his emotional depth, vast repertoire, and enduring classics like 'Ey Reqîb' and 'Le Le Min Dilo.'

name: Hozan Dîno
bio: Renowned Kurdish dengbêj (traditional storyteller and singer) from Turkey, celebrated for preserving and performing epic Kurdish oral traditions. His powerful, raw vocal style keeps centuries-old tales of love, loss, and resistance alive.

name: Hozan Serhad
bio: Hozan Serhad (Süleyman Alpdoğan) was a legendary Kurdish revolutionary musician and guerrilla fighter known for his powerful tembûr playing and his anthems of resistance. He dedicated his art to the Kurdish freedom struggle and was killed in 1999 at age 28, becoming an enduring symbol of Kurdish cultural rebellion.

name: Keyhan Kelhor
bio: Exceptional Kurdish kamancheh (spiked fiddle) virtuoso from Iran (born 1963), known for his deeply expressive playing and technical mastery. He has won multiple international awards and brought Kurdish classical music to global audiences.

name: Leyla Ferîqî
bio: Prominent Kurdish female singer and political activist from Iranian Kurdistan (born 1972). Her voice became a symbol of Kurdish resistance, and she faced imprisonment and persecution for using music to challenge oppression.

name: Merziye Ferîqî
bio: Kurdish singer known for her emotional ballads and strong ties to the Kurdish freedom movement. Her songs express the pain of displacement, political struggle, and hope for justice.

name: Mucteba Mîrzade
bio: Mucteba Mîrzade (Mojtaba Mirzadeh in Persian) was an Iranian - Kurdish violin, kamancheh, and setar master who was influential in Persian classical music, classical and folk Kurdish music, and also in Iranian pop music.

name: Nasir Rezazî
bio: Prominent Kurdish classical vocalist from Iran, recognized for his flawless technique and deep interpretations of Kurdish and Persian classical repertoires. He has performed internationally, elevating Kurdish maqam singing.

name: Şivan Perwer
bio: Legendary Kurdish dengbêj, tembûr player, and political activist (born 1955), often called the voice of Kurdish national identity. With hundreds of songs and decades of exile, he remains an iconic figure in Kurdish music and resistance.

name: Delîla
bio: Delila, born Şenay Gücer in 1981 in Amed, was a Kurdish singer, drummer, and partisan fighter for the freedom of Kurdistan. She was killed by soldiers of the Turkish army on August 25, 2008, in the region of Dêrahînê.
